title: Unexpected Result
description:
## Bug Report

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```sql
CREATE TABLE t1 (c0 int, c1 int);
INSERT INTO t1 VALUES (1, 0), (1, 0);
CREATE TABLE t2 (c0 int, c1 int);
INSERT INTO t2 VALUES (1, 0);
```

```sql
SELECT
    c0
FROM
    t2 ref_0
WHERE
    ref_0.c1 IN (
        SELECT t1.c1
        FROM t2 JOIN t1
        WHERE ref_0.c1 OR TRUE
    ) -- {1}
```
This query returns one row, but it should return two rows. Within the subquery, ` SELECT t1.c1 FROM t2 JOIN t1 WHERE ref_0.c1 OR TRUE` should return `{0}, {0}`, so `IN` expression should always be true. However, the final query only returns one row, instead of two rows.


### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)
{1},{1}


### 3. What did you see instead (Required)
{1}

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

```bash
~/.tiup/bin/tiup playground nightly
```
Version:
```bash
8.0.11-TiDB-v9.0.0-beta.1.pre-563-gea52376
```
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t3 (c0 int, c1 varchar(10) NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE t4 (c0 int, c1 int DEFAULT 0);
SELECT c0 FROM t4 WHERE c1 IN (SELECT c1 FROM t3 WHERE c0 > 1);
key_bug_features: Subquery returns incorrect row count in IN expression
